expert_rss.title: Iran’s Use of Information Warfare in the Conflict against the U.S. and Israel
expert_rss.summary:
One of the most surprising aspects of the current war with Iran has been the Iranians’ effective use of information warfare against the United States and Israel. Iran has waged information warfare in myriad ways, with some efforts dedicated to confusing its adversaries, and some earmarked for domestic consumption. Other aspects have been extremely unorthodox […]

expert_rss.title: An End to the U.S.-Iran War Proves Elusive
expert_rss.summary:
A second round of U.S.-Iran negotiations in Islamabad, that U.S. President Donald Trump predicted would produce a ground-breaking accord and end the nearly two-month war, did not take place earlier this week. The collapse of the meeting, on the eve of the expiration of a two-week ceasefire, rendered the chances of renewed U.S.-Iran warfare as […]

expert_rss.title: Russian Internet Restrictions Undermine Economy and Spark Protests
expert_rss.summary:
Executive Summary: In recent months, the Russian government has expanded internet restrictions to unprecedented levels, surpassing those of the Great Firewall in the People’s Republic of China (PRC) (see EDM, July 24, 2025). In the PRC, mobile internet is not being shut down, and many users can bypass restrictions using virtual private network (VPN) technology […]

expert_rss.title: Putin Rapidly Expands FSB Powers as His Public Support Declines
expert_rss.summary:
Executive Summary: Over the past 25 years, Russian President Vladimir Putin has built and maintained power by serving as the final arbiter of policy, choosing between the Russian Federal Security Service (FSB) and other groups that favor a less repressive approach. He is confident he has enough public support to choose, which reinforces the belief […]
The post Putin Rapidly Expands FSB Power

expert_rss.summary:

Putin effectively issued an ultimatum to Yerevan, demanding that it choose sides in Armenia’s foreign policy. The warning highlighted Armenia’s dilemma in practicing a balanced foreign policy. A cornerstone of Armenia’s […]

Russia, however, does not appear to have abandoned efforts to preserve, if not fully normalize, bilateral ties. expert_rss.summary:

expert_rss.summary: Over the last few years, a clear and troubling pattern has crystallised: where the state fails to manage water and energy scarcity, violent non-state actors step in. Although the link between climate stress and violence is mediated by pre-existing socio-economic fragilities (climate as a threat multiplier), a deeper structural shift is now underway. expert_rss.title: Hizbullah-Affiliated News Outlet Promotes New Digital Media Platform Available On Instagram, Facebook, Telegram, And X
expert_rss.summary: According to a special report published yesterday by the MEMRI Jihad and Terrorism Threat Monitor (JTTM), a Lebanese Hizbullah-affiliated website is promoting a new digital media platform that aims to provide a single source of authoritative news for Lebanon.

expert_rss.title: Québec City Man Faces Terrorism Charges Tied to 764 Extremist Network
expert_rss.summary: RCMP Federal Policing, Eastern Region has announced the arrest and laying of charges against an individual who allegedly promoted the violent ideology of terrorist entity 764 through a group on Telegram.

expert_rss.title: Andrew Vanjani Sworn in as USCIS Chief Information Officer
expert_rss.summary: U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) has sworn in Andrew Vanjani as its new Chief Information Officer, marking his entry into the Senior Executive Service and a leadership role at the center of the agency’s technology operations.

expert_rss.title: Iran Conflict Maritime Update: Export Declines, IRGC Seizures, and Rising Instability Reshape Hormuz Shipping Patterns
expert_rss.summary: Key Takeaways – April 23, 2026 Kharg Island exports fall to ~3M barrels, well below the ~8M weekly average. IRGC attacks and seizure of vessels on April 22 raise the total incidents to 34. Transit remains unstable, with activity fluctuating sharply between April 20 and 22. Bandar Abbas shows heavy congestion, with the majority of vessels operating without AIS. A […]

expert_rss.title: Department of the Navy Releases FY27 Budget Request
expert_rss.summary: The Department of the Navy has released their Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 President’s Budget request focused on restoring American maritime dominance with a total Navy topline of $377.5B, an increase of more than $70.B compared to last year.

expert_rss.title: New White House Directive Pushes Shift Toward Commercial Technology in Federal Acquisition
expert_rss.summary: Last week’s directive from the White House is more than a policy update but a long-overdue shift in how the federal government approaches technology.

expert_rss.title: Iran Says It Has Seized Two Ships in Strait of Hormuz After U.S. Extends Ceasefire
expert_rss.summary: Iran’s navy on Wednesday said it had seized two container ships in the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, shortly after the U.S. extended the ceasefire and as diplomats seek to bring the countries together for peace talks.

expert_rss.title: DOT Withholds $73 Million from New York Over Commercial Driver Licensing Compliance Issues
expert_rss.summary: U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean P. Duffy has announced that the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) is withholding over $73 million from the State of New York for failing to revoke illegally issued non-domiciled commercial learner’s permits (CLPs) and commercial driver’s licenses (CDLs).

expert_rss.title: Personal Property Activity Engages Industry Partners Ahead of Peak PCS Season
expert_rss.summary: The War Department's newest special activity — the Personal Property Activity — met with moving, shipping and logistics leaders to provide an update on the accelerated reform aimed at improving one of the most critical quality-of-life issues facing military families, the permanent change of station moving process.

expert_rss.title: Thousands of Kids Descend on Pentagon for 'Bring Your Child to Work Day'
expert_rss.summary: More than 8,000 children visited the Pentagon for "Bring Your Child to Work Day," where they explored military displays, met with leaders like Secretary Pete Hegseth, and participated in a special reading with Second Lady Usha Vance.

expert_rss.title: Air Force Pararescuemen Complete Combat Leader Course
expert_rss.summary: Air Force pararescuemen assigned to the 68th Rescue Squadron completed the Combat Leader Course at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base, Ariz., enhancing their operational readiness and leadership skills.

expert_rss.title: Southcom Establishes Autonomous Warfare Command
expert_rss.summary: Marine Corps Gen. Francis L. Donovan directed the establishment of the Southcom Autonomous Warfare Command, which will employ autonomous, semiautonomous and unmanned platforms and systems to counter threats and challenges across the region.

expert_rss.title: Space Force Delivers Final GPS III Satellite to Orbit
expert_rss.summary: The Space Force's Space Systems Command and Combat Forces Command successfully delivered the GPS III-8 satellite into orbit, providing GPS capabilities to the warfighter that are three times as accurate and eight times as resistant to jamming as the previous constellation.

expert_rss.title: Braker Breakthrough: Drone-Delivered Bunker-Busting Warhead Passes Test
expert_rss.summary: Army infantry drone operators successfully tested a new warhead designed for delivery by an unmanned aerial system, a significant leap forward for battlefield technology only weeks after the initial design and rapid prototyping of the system.

expert_rss.title: Saildrone’s Missile-Toting Spectre Enters Navy’s Medium-Sized Unmanned Ship Competition
expert_rss.summary:
Saildrone has joined forces with defense juggernauts to supersize its proven vehicle concept and equip it with missiles and sub-hunting sensors. 
The post Saildrone’s Missile-Toting Spectre Enters Navy’s Medium-Sized Unmanned Ship Competition appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Trump Puts Out Kill Order On Iran’s Small Boats (Updated)
expert_rss.summary:
Trump also said he is going to "triple" demining efforts in the Strait, although it is unclear what, if any, of these activities are currently happening at all.
The post Trump Puts Out Kill Order On Iran’s Small Boats (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: NASA’s 777 Flying Laboratory Touches Down At Langley
expert_rss.summary:
NASA’s largest research aircraft will take over from the agency’s iconic DC-8, with a first test campaign planned for 2027.
The post NASA’s 777 Flying Laboratory Touches Down At Langley appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Navy Rushing To Arm Carrier Strike Groups With Hellfire Missiles
expert_rss.summary:
The Navy is pushing more counter-drone hard-kill capabilities to its fleet as it comes to terms with the growing threat of one-way attack drones.
The post Navy Rushing To Arm Carrier Strike Groups With Hellfire Missiles appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Iranian Attacks Change Way Navy Refuels Its Ships In Middle East
expert_rss.summary:
The Navy created a commercial "tanker treadmill" to top off its oilers out at sea, offering lessons that could have larger impacts on operations in the future.
The post Iranian Attacks Change Way Navy Refuels Its Ships In Middle East appeared first on The War Zone.

expert_rss.title: Iran Ramps Up Attacks On Ships In The Strait Of Hormuz After Trump Ceasefire Extension (Updated)
expert_rss.summary:
With Iran refusing to commit to peace talks, the future of the ceasefire remains in doubt.
The post Iran Ramps Up Attacks On Ships In The Strait Of Hormuz After Trump Ceasefire Extension (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.
